title: Dryopteris namegatae
taxonomy: Dryopteris namegatae is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, which includes about 850 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is commonly known as the wood ferns. It is native to Japan, and its specific classification can be further broken down to the kingdom Plantae, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. This species, like others in the family, is characterized by its simple leaves and spore-producing structures. The classification of Dryopteris namegatae reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
